title: Adicea urticifolia
intro:
There is no information available on "Adicea urticifolia". However, I found a plant called "Adina urticifolia" which is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. 

Here's a paragraph on Adina urticifolia:

Adina urticifolia is a plant species native to tropical Asia, commonly known for its medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Adina urticifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Adina urticifolia is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Adicea urticifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The genus Adicea is part of the family Rubiaceae, which is a family of flowering plants that also includes coffee and gardenias. Adicea urticifolia is native to the island of Madagascar. It is a shrub or small tree that produces small white flowers. Adicea urticifolia is classified in the kingdom Plantae, family Rubiaceae, genus Adicea.
